key_insight: Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS) uses prior-guided symbolic programs plus evolutionary search and gradient fitting to discover interpretable PDE representations beyond numerical tables or black-box neural models.
engineering_relevance: This offers engineers and applied scientists a way to extract compact, human-readable PDE laws or approximations that can support analysis, interpretation, and downstream model design.
method: Agent translates PDE theory, problem constraints, and prior search experience into differentiable symbolic programs.
Evolutionary search refines the symbolic form while gradient-based optimization fits continuous parameters.
Acts as inductive-bias injection rather than blind symbolic regression.
evidence_signal: Across five PDE problems spanning bounded dynamics, finite-time blow-up, and free-boundary focusing, the method recovered known analytic forms and produced new interpretable laws such as an Allen-Cahn interface formula and a nine-parameter Keller-Segel contraction law.
watch_item: The approach likely depends on strong prior guidance and search quality, so performance may degrade on problems with weak theoretical constraints or highly ambiguous symbolic structure.
paper_title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
